---
title: "How to Play"
description: "Controls, the objective, and the moment-to-moment battle loop."
---

You command the [**blue army**]{.army-blue} (top of the field). Your objective:
defeat the [**red army**]{.army-red} (bottom). A side loses when it has no units
left fighting --- every remaining unit is dead or routing.

```{=html}
<figure class="demo">
  <video class="demo-video" controls muted loop playsinline preload="metadata"
         poster="media/clash.jpg">
    <source src="media/clash.mp4" type="video/mp4">
    Your browser can't play this clip --- see the
    <a href="https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta">repository</a> to run the game.
  </video>
  <figcaption>Selecting units and issuing move/attack orders during a battle.</figcaption>
</figure>
```

## Controls

| Action | Control |
| --- | --- |
| Select a unit | **Left-click** |
| Select multiple | **Left-click and drag** a box |
| Move | **Right-click** the ground |
| Attack | **Right-click** an enemy unit |
| Pan camera | <kbd>W</kbd><kbd>A</kbd><kbd>S</kbd><kbd>D</kbd> / arrow keys / screen edges |
| Zoom | **Mouse wheel** |
| Active pause | <kbd>Space</kbd> |

A right-click on empty ground is a **move** order; a right-click on an enemy is an
**attack** order. Selection and camera stay live even while paused, so you can plan
under pressure.

## The battle loop

1. **Select** --- left-click a unit, or drag a box to grab several at once. The info
   panel fills with the selected unit's stats.
2. **Order** --- right-click to send them. Move them onto an enemy's flank, or
   right-click the enemy directly to attack.
3. **Watch the bars** --- each unit shows a strength bar that drops as it takes
   casualties. Morale matters as much as numbers (see [Tactics](tactics.qmd)).
4. **Exploit the break** --- when a unit **routs**, it flees (shown faded) and stops
   counting toward the battle. Routs spread to nearby allies, so a single break can
   cascade.
5. **Win** --- eliminate or rout the entire enemy army and a **Victory** overlay
   appears, with **Fight Again** to restart.

## Recording & replays

Every battle is recorded automatically --- a `● REC` indicator shows top-center. When
a battle ends it's saved, and you can:

- **Watch Replay** --- re-run the battle you just played (`▶ REPLAY`). You can still
  pan and inspect units during playback, but can't issue new orders.
- **Load Replay** --- open any earlier saved battle from a file picker (top-right,
  also on the end screen).
- **Fight Again** --- start a fresh, newly-recorded battle.

See **[Replays](replays.qmd)** for how the deterministic system works and why the
logs are tiny.

---

*Source: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/README.md)
in the repository.*
